title: limonium beaumierianum var. tripeaui
intro: Limonium beaumierianum var. tripeaui, commonly known as sea lavender, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, reducing pain, and relieving respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of sea lavender have been attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as limonene and linalool, offering health benefits like reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, sea lavender is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
